== Infobox ==
{{infobox religious building

 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.751333333333335|N|136.96502777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity = {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}
 | type = Former Village shrine

 | festival = (Not specified)

 | founded_by = {{ill|Endō Yoshitaka|qid=Q11642251|1=Endō Yoshitaka}}

== Overview ==
'''Atago Shrine (Gujo)''' (愛宕神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Yahata-cho, Gujo City, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It was originally founded in 1600 by local warlords and later integrated into the modern shrine Ranking system.
== History ==
Tradition holds that it was founded in Keichō 5 (1600) by Yamana Kotarō, though various legends exist regarding its origins. In later centuries, the shrine was revered for its role in fire prevention and was supported by local pilgrimage groups.
